comments.body: Done. executor.input_schema_for is the single resolver; editor._config_input_schema + interpreter._consumer_input_schema both delegate. 323 tests green, lint clean, behavior unchanged.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS**

All three acceptance criteria are met by the implementation:

1. **Single shared helper** — `executor.input_schema_for` (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:206–235`) is the one resolver handling both scripted (via `contract_for`) and agent (via `get_prompt.inputs`) kinds. Its docstring explicitly notes it is "the SINGLE resolver shared by the runtime validator (interpreter) and the edit-time validator (editor) so the two can never diverge (story b642)."

2. **Both `validate_consumer_input` and `validate_node_config` delegate to it** — no parallel reimplementations:
   - `interpreter._consumer_input_schema` (`interpreter.py:66–76`) extracts the action and calls `input_schema_for`; `validate_consumer_input` (`interpreter.py:95`) calls `_consumer_input_schema`.
   - `editor._config_input_schema` (`editor.py:262–267`) calls `input_schema_for`; `validate_node_config` (`editor.py:300`) calls `_config_input_schema`.
   - Neither module contains any independent re-implementation of the schema-name resolution logic.

3. **Tests exist for the validation behavior** — `tests/unit/workflow/test_editor_validate.py` covers all four defined axes of `validate_node_config` (empty, malformed, violation, validator failure). Behavior is unchanged since both modules now share the same resolver path.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Code review (M2) found contract input-schema resolution duplicated across modules,
risking edit-time vs runtime divergence.

## What / Scope
Consolidate the "resolve a step/prompt's INPUT schema name" logic currently repeated in
`interpreter._consumer_input_schema`, `editor._config_input_schema`, and the field
extraction in `editor._schema_fields` into ONE shared helper, used by both the runtime
validator (interpreter) and the edit-time validator (editor).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A single shared helper resolves a step/prompt input schema name (scripted via contract_for, agent via get_prompt.inputs).
- [ ] `interpreter.validate_consumer_input` and `editor.validate_node_config` both use it (no parallel reimplementations).
- [ ] Existing validation tests stay green; behavior unchanged.
compiled_state.title: Consolidate duplicated contract input-schema resolution (editor + interpreter)
